old: 这里可以存放行李。
new: 他把现金存放在银行保险柜里。
reason: Sentence illustrates 'to store/leave for safekeeping,' not 'to deposit'; the definition for this row is 'to deposit' so the sentence should match.

old: You can store your luggage here.
new: He deposited his cash in the bank safe.
reason: Matches corrected sentence.

old: 他们终于达成了目标。
new: 他们终于达成了协议。
reason: 达成目标 is an unnatural collocation; 达成 collocates with 协议/共识/协定, not 目标. 实现目标 is the standard phrase for reaching a goal.

old: They finally reached their goal.
new: They finally reached an agreement.
reason: Matches corrected sentence.

old: 这件事要单独处理。
new: 这件事要单列一份报告。
reason: Sentence uses 单独 (a different headword) rather than 单; the headword 单 does not appear in the sentence.

old: This matter should be handled separately.
new: This matter should have a separate report.
reason: Matches corrected sentence using headword 单.

old: I want to talk with you one-on-one.
new: I want to talk with you alone.
reason: 'One-on-one' does not match the definition 'by oneself'; 'alone' (privately, just us) better reflects 单独 in this sentence.

old: 他把我当成了他的朋友。
new: 他把这次考试当成了一次练习。
reason: Cross-row near-duplicate with row 3265 sentence_1 ('他把我当作朋友'); same headword, same scenario of treating a person as a friend.

old: He regarded me as his friend.
new: He considered this exam a practice run.
reason: Matches corrected sentence.

old: 信用卡下个月到期。
new: 贷款下个月到期。
reason: Credit cards expire (过期) rather than become due (到期); 到期 applies to scheduled obligations like loan payments, not cards.

old: The credit card becomes due next month.
new: The loan becomes due next month.
reason: Matches corrected sentence.

old: 租约下个月到期，要续签。
new: 租约下个月到期，要续约。
reason: 续签 is not in the L5 vocabulary; 续约 is the appropriate alternative.

old: The lease becomes due next month and needs to be renewed.
new: The lease comes due next month and needs to be renewed.
reason: Minor phrasing fix to match revised sentence.

old: 牛奶到期了，不能喝了。
new: 护照两年后到期。
reason: Food items use 过期 not 到期; 到期 is for scheduled deadlines on documents or contracts.

old: The milk has expired and can no longer be drunk.
new: The passport expires in two years.
reason: Matches corrected sentence.

old: 优惠券已经到期，不能使用了。
new: 他的签证已经到期，不能使用了。
reason: 优惠券 (coupon) is not in the L5 vocabulary; 签证 is within scope.

old: The coupon has already expired and cannot be used.
new: His visa has already expired and cannot be used.
reason: Matches corrected sentence.

old: He bowed his head and conceded to his opponent.
new: He yielded to his opponent.
reason: 'Bowed his head' reflects the literal sense (row 3287), not the figurative 'to yield' definition this row illustrates.

old: 飞机在地面上等候。
new: 孩子们坐在地面上玩耍。
reason: Sentences 1 and 2 both feature an airplane on the ground — near-duplicate scenario within the same row.

old: The airplane is waiting on the ground.
new: The children sat on the ground and played.
reason: Matches corrected sentence.

old: 他们一起度过了一天。
new: 他在国外度过了童年。
reason: Sentence_2 and sentence_3 are near-duplicates in structure: both describe a group spending a time period together in a location (度过了+time).

old: They spent a day together.
new: He spent his childhood abroad.
reason: Matches corrected sentence and adds structural variety.
